_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The California Regulatory Notice Register is published weekly by the Office of Administrative Law (OAL), an executive-branch agency responsible for ensuring California state agencies comply with the rulemaking procedures and standards set forth in California's Administrative Procedure Act (APA), California Government Code § 11340 et seq. The January 2024 registers are not themselves binding regulations; they are the official public notification vehicle by which agencies announce their intent to adopt, amend, or repeal regulations in the California Code of Regulations (CCR). Once a Notice of Proposed Action is published, a minimum 45-day public comment period is opened, after which the agency must submit a completed rulemaking file to OAL within one year of the publication date (Government Code § 11346.4(b)).

For the workspace — operating as a licensed freight forwarder with road, air, and ocean movements into and out of California — the compliance-chain placement is that of a **regulated operator and indirect subject of proposed rulemakings**, not a rulemaking party. The workspace does not file notices or appear in the register as a proposing agency. Rather, it must monitor notices relevant to its operating licenses, vehicle fleet (Title 13 – DMV), labor practices (Title 8, Title 15), environmental operations (Title 13 – Air Resources Board), and vendor/subcontractor obligations (FPPC conflict-of-interest codes affecting public-agency counterparties). Any proposed regulation published in the January 2024 registers that reaches final adoption and is filed with the Secretary of State will become binding California law approximately 30 days after OAL approval, directly affecting the workspace's compliance posture in California. Legal role confirmation required: the workspace should obtain counsel confirmation of whether any of its California-based operations trigger participation obligations (e.g., written comment standing) in open dockets identified in the January 2024 registers.

*Source: California Regulatory Notice Register, OAL, January 5, 2024. https://oal.ca.gov/2024/01/05/january-2024-california-regulatory-notice-registers/ | About the Regular Rulemaking Process, OAL. https://oal.ca.gov/rulemaking_participation/*
